title: CephFS as backing storage
body:
It would be great if we could use CephFS for rootless podman storage. If I understand correctly, network file systems usually are not supported because they don't support id-mapped mounts?
It however seems that CephFS does support this: https://man7.org/linux/man-pages/man2/mount_setattr.2.html#NOTES

Would it be possible to use it as backing storage?

comments.body:
@giuseppe PTAL

(Just so that there is _some_ substantial response here, I read the error checks to mean that native id-mapping is not a blocker — that can be done by copies — but generally using locally-allocated subUID/subGID ownership values for files stored on a network filesystem seems hard to use from any other computer that could mount the same filesystem. So, the code enforces using a FUSE filesystem instead, and storing the ownership information in extended attributes. But I’m not familiar with the problem space and it’s very likely I’m missing something.)

idmapped mounts are restricted to root, so they are not very helpful for rootless.

On a network file system you could use fuse-overlays (assuming cephFS supports extended attributes).

Maybe I was mislead by FHGFSFs being dropped from the list of network file systems in this commit:
https://github.com/containers/storage/commit/be2b188bc68645ed43b837d45dfa7ca8f17b42d3

I'll try fuse-overlayfs then. My real issue is that permissions for new files inside the containers end up being messed up, and I attributed that to the following warning:
`WARN[0000] Network file system detected as backing store.  Enforcing overlay option `force_mask="700"`.  Add it to storage.conf to silence this warning`
